creator: mi@FreeBSD.org
text:
Trying to build the port after it was updated I get:

....
cc -c -fno-strict-aliasing -O2 -pipe -march=core2   -fstack-protector -fno-strict-aliasing -DNDEBUG  -I. -IInclude -I./Include -I/opt/include -fPIC -DPy_BUILD_CORE -o Modules/config.o Modules/config.c
Modules/config.c:37:1: error: expected identifier or '('
/MARKER 2/i
^
Modules/config.c:64:17: error: use of undeclared identifier 'PyMarshal_Init'
    {"marshal", PyMarshal_Init},
                ^
2 errors generated.
*** [Modules/config.o] Error code 1

Indeed, looking inside the Modules/config.c, I see the following lines around line 37:

....
extern void init_symtable(void);
extern void initxxsubtype(void);
/MARKER 2/i
/MARKER 2/i
/MARKER 2/i
{"thread", initthread},
{"signal", initsignal},
....

Looks like the configure-process fails to properly update the file... Tried this without PYMALLOC (as I usually build Python) as well as with all the config-options set to default values:

===> The following configuration options are available for python27-2.7.9:
     DEBUG=off: Build with debugging support
     IPV6=off: IPv6 protocol support
     LIBFFI=on: Use libffi from ports instead of bundled version
     NLS=on: Enable gettext support for the locale module
     PYMALLOC=on: Enable specialized mallocs
     SEM=on: POSIX semaphores support
     THREADS=on: Threading support
====> Unicode support: you have to select exactly one of them
     UCS2=off: Enable UCS2 Unicode Strings
     UCS4=on: Enable UCS4 Unicode Strings

Please, advise.
